label: Media
title: Media | Staten Island Oral Surgery
meta_description: Stay updated with our latest news, events, and breakthroughs in the field of oral and maxillofacial surgery. From patient success stories to insights...
main_h1: Staten Island Oral & Maxillofacial Surgery Media & Videos
hero_intro: Stay updated with our latest news, events, and breakthroughs in the field of oral and maxillofacial surgery. From patient success stories to insights from our expert team, our media coverage provides an engaging window into our ongoing dedication to excellence in patient care.

content_sections.heading: Dr. Hoffman Prepares To Remove Tumor To Help Young Girl Smile Again
paragraphs: Jannet Sylva is a 12-year-old girl who just arrived from Gambia. Jannet had a severe facial deformity from a benign tumor of the mandible that has changed her entire facial appearance.

content_sections.heading: Staten Island Oral & Maxillofacial Surgery
paragraphs: Staten Island Oral & Maxillofacial Surgery specializes in caring for patients with a variety of problems of the mouth, teeth, and facial regions including painful wisdom teeth, complex jaw deformities, or missing teeth.

content_sections.heading: Dental Implant Testimonial
paragraphs: Patients share their experiences of having their missing teeth replaced with Dental Implants by Dr. David Hoffman in Staten Island, New York. Staten Island Oral & Maxillofacial Surgery offers dental implants in NYC that look, feel, and function like natural teeth.

content_sections.heading: Parent's Guide For Cleft Lip & Palate
paragraphs: Parents share their experiences of having a child in need of cleft lip and palate surgery and their decision to have Staten Island oral surgeon Dr. David Hoffman perform the surgery.

content_sections.heading: Cleft Lip And Palate Surgery
paragraphs: Dr. Hoffman of Staten Island Oral Surgery performs cleft lip and palate surgery. Staten Island Oral Surgery provides Cleft Lip and Palate surgery for patients in Staten Island, New York City, and New Jersey. Dr. Hoffman is Medical Director of Healing the Children, Missions Abroad Programs, a non-profit organization dedicated to the surgical treatment of children in Third World countries.

content_sections.heading: Unlocking The Jaw - Part 1
paragraphs: Our oral surgeons of Staten Island Oral Surgery perform surgery to unlock the fused jaws of 2 young patients on Discovery Health. Each patient underwent Distraction Osteogenesis in Staten Island, New York to correct deformities in their jaws.

content_sections.heading: Unlocking The Jaw - Part 2
paragraphs: Our oral surgeons continue surgery on 2 young patients. Each patient underwent jaw surgery to correct deformities in their jaws that prevented them from opening their mouths.

content_sections.heading: Healing The Children's Medical Mission In Colombia
paragraphs: Colombian children smile again thanks to Healing the Children. Healing the Children is a volunteer, non-profit organization that provides health care to Latin American children and children of Third World Countries. Dr. David Hoffman serves as the Medical Director for Healing the Children and has led over forty surgical missions to Latin American countries. He has performed surgery on more than 1000 children with cleft lip, cleft, palate, and other facial deformities.

content_sections.heading: Healing The Children - Cleft Palate Speech Therapy
paragraphs: This video shows the before and after results that speech therapy can have for children with cleft palate. The video was filmed in Neiva, Colombia for the Healing the Children medical mission. Healing the Children is a volunteer, non-profit organization that provides health care to Latin American children and children of Third World Countries. Staten Island Oral and Maxillofacial Surgeon, Dr. David Hoffman, is the mission’s surgical director, and has been at the forefront of bringing cleft lip and palate surgery to developing countries in Latin America for more than 20 years and has personally operated on over 200 children.

content_sections.heading: Corrective Jaw Surgery
paragraphs: The team at Staten Island Oral Surgery performed jaw surgery to help this patient regain function following a serious car accident that fused his jaw shut. Oral surgeons used advanced CT technology to perform a series of complicated jaw surgeries to free the jaw and install custom-made joints.

content_sections.heading: She was shot in the face at age 6, now Staten Island doctors have given her beautiful smile back
paragraphs: Marzia was just 6 years old and hiding with her family from the Taliban in Afghanistan when a targeted, violent Talibani ambush took the life of her father and brother.

Though she survived the firearms assault, bullets shattered her upper jaw, destroyed her right eye and left her with few remaining teeth. Years of pain, bullying and seclusion followed as she hid with her family in her impoverished village while the war in Afghanistan raged on.

But inside the cheerful offices of Staten Island Oral and Maxillofacial Surgery in Bloomfield, the beautiful 19-year-old girl received the life-changing surgery that gave her back her smile — just in time for Christmas.

content_sections.heading: A Bathroom Disaster, a Bubble Bath Mix-Up, and the Dental Implant Commercial That Has Staten Island Talking
content_sections.subheading: Award-winning director teams up with his father, Dr. David Hoffman, to create a hilarious new spot for Staten Island Oral & Maxillofacial Surgery — now airing locally before a planned national rollout
paragraphs: Picture this: a middle-aged man on a promising dinner date. The evening is going well — maybe too well. As the chemistry heats up, he excuses himself to the bathroom. What happens next is sixty seconds of escalating catastrophe that has Staten Island residents laughing, cringing, and quietly calling their dentist.

The new television commercial for Staten Island Oral & Maxillofacial Surgery — now airing exclusively on Staten Island — opens on that romantic date before following our hapless hero behind a closed bathroom door. Once inside, his stomach betrays him. Loudly. He scrambles to turn on the bath faucet, hoping the rushing water will mask the noise. In the process, he knocks over a bottle of bubble bath, and the tub begins filling with a cascade of foamy bubbles. After composing himself, he strikes a match and lights a candle to handle the lingering odor. Then the real crisis arrives: the toilet overflows and refuses to flush. He grabs the plunger and gets to work.

At that exact moment, the bathroom door swings open. His date steps in — and sees what appears to be the most romantic scene imaginable. Candlelight flickering softly. A bubble bath drawn and waiting. She is charmed. But our hero has a bigger problem: as he frantically plunges the toilet, his denture pops loose and drops straight into the bowl. Startled by her entrance, he does the unthinkable — he fishes it out and jams it right back into his mouth. She reaches for the light switch, dims the room, and leans in for a kiss. He turns away.
Then the tagline lands: “Don’t let your teeth ruin the moment.”

The spot is the brainchild of the director, an award-winning commercial director whose résumé includes several Super Bowl advertisements, Apple TV campaigns, and other nationally recognized productions. He also happens to be the son of Dr. David C. Hoffman, the founder and lead surgeon at Staten Island Oral & Maxillofacial Surgery. The father-son collaboration brought a level of storytelling and production value rarely seen in local advertising.

“Hi son understood exactly what we wanted to say — that living with dentures or missing teeth affects real moments in people’s lives, moments that should be joyful,” said Dr. Hoffman. “He took that message and turned it into something people actually want to watch. I couldn’t be prouder of what he created.”

The commercial transitions from comedy to a clear message about a procedure that is changing lives: Teeth-in-a-Day, also known as All-on-4 or All-on-X dental implants. The revolutionary technique replaces an entire arch of teeth permanently using just four to six implants — all placed in a single visit. Within 24 hours, patients walk out with a fixed, natural-looking set of teeth, leaving the frustrations of loose dentures, adhesive creams, and dietary restrictions behind for good.

“People don’t realize how dramatically this procedure can change their quality of life,” Dr. Hoffman added. “We’re not just restoring teeth — we’re restoring confidence, nutrition, and the simple ability to smile without thinking twice. Our in-house lab and surgical center allow us to handle the entire process under one roof, from planning to final restoration.”

Staten Island Oral & Maxillofacial Surgery, staffed by Dr. David C. Hoffman, D.D.S., F.A.C.S., along with Dr. Lydia J. Lam, Dr. Jessica Li and Dr. Monika Patel, operates a state-of-the-art facility that includes an AAAHC (Quad A) accredited operating suite, an in-house dental laboratory, and the latest implant technology. The practice has built its reputation on providing hospital-level surgical care in a comfortable, modern office setting.

The commercial is currently airing exclusively on Staten Island, giving the borough a first look before a planned national rollout. Residents interested in learning more about Teeth-in-a-Day or All-on-4 dental implants can schedule a free consultation by calling 718-226-1251 or visiting statenislandoralsurgery.us and iwantteeth.com.
